title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Product Reasoning Brief: Prototype Fidelity
summary: A team starts production architecture while desirability, usability, feasibility, viability, safety, and operating assumptions remain untested. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 prototype fidelity increment in ranked assumption map and prototype evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Prototype Fidelity · Theory Prototype Fidelity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Prototype Fidelity contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Prototype Fidelity · Theory Prototype Fidelity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment. For Predict Prototype Fidelity · Theory Prototype Fidelity, keep Prototype Fidelity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Prototype Fidelity while producing ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment. A defensible solution applies Prototype Fidelity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-prototype-fidelity — Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
focus: pp1-prototype-fidelity
decision-evidence: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Defend the Prototype Fidelity model · Theory Prototype Fidelity
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Prototype Fidelity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.

Evidence target: Defend the Prototype Fidelity model · Theory Prototype Fidelity.
steps.why: Retrieving Prototype Fidelity through the concrete ranked assumption map and prototype evidence case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Prototype Fidelity model · Theory Prototype Fidelity, keep Prototype Fidelity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
